title: Worker client: GIMPS-style checkpoint/restart and client-side self-checking arithmetic
context: PLAN.md v0.2 adds two new M2 exit criteria to the worker client: (1) GIMPS-style checkpoint/restart — periodic state saves that allow interrupted laptop-class sessions to resume without losing work; and (2) client-side self-checking arithmetic — mod-p residue checks per chunk, analogous to GIMPS's Gerbicz check, that catch most bad results before they consume verification budget on the coordinator. These were not in the original worker-012 task scope (consent, resource caps, signing, result submission) and represent distinct reliability and efficiency improvements. Depends on worker-012.
objective: Extend the donated-compute worker client with GIMPS-style checkpoint/restart (periodic state saves for resumable sessions) and client-side self-checking arithmetic (mod-p residue checks per chunk, analogous to the Gerbicz check) to catch bad results before coordinator verification.
acceptanceCriteria: Worker client saves checkpoints at regular intervals (configurable frequency) so an interrupted session can resume from the last checkpoint without redoing prior work; checkpoint files contain no secrets or PII.

Worker client runs mod-p residue self-checks (analogous to GIMPS's Gerbicz check) at the end of each chunk computation and refuses to submit a result that fails the self-check, logging the failure without revealing secrets.
Checkpoint/restart and self-check behavior are covered by tests; the verification gate on the coordinator side is unchanged.
No secrets in logs or artifacts; MIT-licensed.
output: A PR adding checkpoint/restart and client-side self-checking arithmetic to the donated-compute worker client.
